messages.content:
Generate a title for a "101" educational article about this "core concept": {{3.CoreConcept}}

===========
STRUCTURE
===========

The title must follow this structure:

1. Start with the primary keyword from the core concept, followed immediately by "101" (must appear within the first 5 words)

2. After the colon, add a benefit-focused subtitle that follows one of these formats:
   - Why [topic] Matters to [audience/outcome]
   - Preventing/Solving [problem/challenge]
   - The [key elements] for [desired outcome]
   - What Every [audience] Should Understand
   - Balancing [factor 1] and [factor 2]
   - Decoding/Understanding [complex aspect]
   - Beyond [basic understanding] to [advanced insight]
   - The Science/Mechanics Behind [observable result]
   - Maximizing [desired benefit]
   - Critical Factors for [business objective]

==========
EXAMPLES
==========

Core concept: "industrial roof insulation options"
Title: "Roof Insulation 101: Why R-Values Matter to Your Energy Budget"

Core concept: "sustainable commercial roofing"
Title: "Sustainable Roofing 101: Balancing Environmental Impact and ROI"

Core concept: "moisture management during coating"
Title: "Moisture Management 101: Preventing the Top Cause of Coating Failures"

Core concept: "industrial roof system selection"
Title: "Roof System Selection 101: Critical Factors for Long-Term Performance in Industrial Applications"

Core concept: "commercial roof coating lifespan"
Title: "Coating Lifespan 101: What Every Building Owner Should Understand About Durability"

Core concept: "solar panels on commercial roofs"
Title: "Solar Roofing 101: Maximizing Energy Returns for Commercial Buildings"

Core concept: "roof warranty documentation"
Title: "Roof Warranties 101: Protecting Your Investment Through Proper Documentation"

Core concept: "regional industrial roof regulations"
Title: "Roofing Regulations 101: Navigating Requirements for Compliance and Cost Savings"

========
OUTPUT
========

Return only the generated title without any additional text, explanation, or markup.

messages.content:
You are a research assistant on a mission to help gather information for a Section of an article about [{{3.CoreConcept}}]. 

{{3.FormatDescription}}

=====
LINK FORMAT
=====

  - Provide complete, unshortened URLs for each source
  - Do not use hyperlinked numbers or abbreviated citations
  - Permalinks are preferred over temporary URLs when available
  - Display each URL on its own line immediately following the relevant information
  - Include the full web address starting with https://
  - For database sources, include the permanent URL (permalink) rather than a session-specific URL

=====
SOURCE BUNDLE FORMAT
=====

For each source bundle, provide:
1. Quote: [1-2 relevant sentences from the source]
2. Source: [Publication/Organization name]
3. Link: [URL]
4. Key Points: 
    - [3-4 bullet points with supporting information]
    - [Include specific numbers/statistics when available]
    - [Include relevant expert names/credentials]

=====
YOUR MISSION
=====

Your mission is to provide SEVEN sources that provide credibility to the title "{{9.result}}" and this section outline:

{{21.result}}

=====
OUTPUT FORMAT
=====

Extract and return only the research results, in plain text, WITHOUT any markup, markdown, additional formatting, or explanation.

IMPORTANT:  If a source bundle is from an excluded domain, return "CITATION NOT FOUND".  Do NOT create a hypothetical source bundle.

EXAMPLES (the following data is fictional, do not use it as a source):

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
SOURCE BUNDLE #A1
1. Quote: "Despite widespread use of traditional roof coatings, commercial buildings still lose an average of 33% of their cooling energy through the roof, with current coating strategies failing to adapt to changing climate conditions."
2. Source: Building Energy Performance Quarterly
3. Link: [URL]
4. Key Points:
- Study analyzed 500 commercial buildings over 3 years
- Traditional coatings show 40% degradation in performance after 5 years
- Dr. James Miller, Energy Systems Engineer, calls for paradigm shift in coating approach
- Current strategies based on outdated climate models

SOURCE BUNDLE #A2
1. Quote: "Post-installation warranty extensions often create a false sense of security, with 64% of extended warranty claims being denied due to maintenance-related exclusions that weren't present in the original warranty terms."
2. Source: Commercial Construction Insurance Review
3. Link: [URL]
4. Key Points:
- Analysis of 2,000 warranty extension claims over 2019-2023
- Average cost of denied claims: $85,000 per incident
- Dr. Rachel Martinez, Risk Management Specialist, warns of "warranty extension trap"
- 78% of building owners unaware of new restrictions in extended coverage

(continue with outputs for Source Bundle #’s A3-A7.  NO OTHER TEXT OR EXPLANATION IS ALLOWED IN THE OUTPUT.)

content.text:
You are a research editor. Your ONLY job is to find and return ONE valid citation from the list of "source bundles" below.

================
SOURCE BUNDLES
================

{{11.choices[].message.content}}

================
ARTICLE CONTEXT
================

The core concept of the article is [{{3.CoreConcept}}].

The title is [{{9.result}}].

And the focus is:  
{{3.`Section1Focus-FUNDAMENTAL-CONCEPTS`}}

================
VALIDATION RULES
================

STEP 1: Domain Check

For each source bundle:

1. Extract the root domain using these rules:
   - Strip protocol (http://, https://)
   - Strip www. if present
   - Take everything up to the first / or end of string
   - Strip any port numbers
   - Remove the TLD (.com, .org, .net, etc)

2. The resulting root domain is INVALID if:
   - It contains any of these forbidden strings as part of any word:
     roof
     contractor
     guild
     service
     taylor
     build
     company
     install
     inc
     llc

Examples of domain extraction and validation:

Full URL → Extracted root domain → Verdict

https://www.cartarga.com/blog/commercial-roofing → cartarga → VALID

https://paint.org/sustainability/cool-roofs → paint → VALID  

https://blog.industryknowhow.com/research → industryknowhow → VALID

https://research-institute.edu/studies → research-institute → VALID

https://tracoroofing.com/case-study → tracoroofing → INVALID (contains "roof")

https://jonesbuilders.com/about → jonesbuilders → INVALID (contains "build")

https://www.roofr.com/blog → roofr → INVALID (contains "roof")

https://pro-service.net/contact → pro-service → INVALID (contains "service")

IF A ROOT DOMAIN IS INVALID, RETURN "CITATION NOT FOUND" and start over with STEP 1 for any additional source bundles.

---

STEP 2:
If multiple source bundles are VALID (they passed Step 1), choose the BEST ONE based on how well it fits the Article Context.

========
OUTPUT
========

If ZERO source bundles have valid root domains, return "CITATION NOT FOUND" and STOP HERE.

Otherwise, return the complete source bundle exactly as formatted in the input.

NO OTHER TEXT OR EXPLANATION IS ALLOWED IN THE OUTPUT.

You are a recognized authority in commercial and industrial roofing with over a decade of hands-on experience. You excel at explaining complex roofing concepts to property owners and facility managers in clear, actionable terms. Your mission is to write Section 1 of an article for an industry publication. ======================== OVERALL ARTICLE CONTEXT ======================== CORE CONCEPT: "{{3.CoreConcept}}" ARTICLE TITLE: "{{9.result}}" ======================== CURRENT SECTION CONTEXT ======================== SECTION TITLE: {{3.Section1Title}} OUTLINE FOR THIS SECTION: {{21.result}} ==================== WRITING PARAMETERS ==================== Primary Audience: Commercial/industrial property owners and facilities managers who need to make informed roofing decisions Tone/Style: Professional - Open each section with urgency/stakes - Keep narrative in third person - Focus on industry impact, not personal experience - Use specific examples rather than anecdotes Technical Level: Clear and accessible (7th-grade reading level) ================= SECTION STRUCTURE ================= 1. Introduction (50-75 words) - Open with a compelling hook that: • Highlights urgency or stakes • Uses concrete examples or statistics • Avoids personal anecdotes - Preview key issues - Bridge to subsections 2. Pull Quote (15-20 words) - Format: "[Issue with current practice] + [Impact/Consequence]" - Must be provocative yet credible - Emphasize contrast between accepted practice and needed change 3. Three Subsections (100-125 words each) For all content: - Use industry knowledge and general facts - Keep technical terms accessible - Connect to the main argument Formatting: - Break ALL content into short paragraphs, including the introduction - Each paragraph should be 2-3 sentences long, with a maximum of 3 sentences - Each subsection should have 4-6 paragraphs - Use

tags around each paragraph ============= OUTPUT FORMAT =============

{{3.Section1Title}}

[Introduction]

[Pull Quote]

[Subsection A Title]

[Subsection A Content]

[Subsection B Title]

[Subsection B Content]

[Subsection C Title]

[Subsection C Content]

LINK FORMAT
=====

=====

1. Quote: [1-2 relevant sentences from the source]

    - [3-4 bullet points with supporting information]

=====

Your mission is to provide SEVEN sources that provide credibility to the title "{{9.result}}" and this section outline:

{{44.result}}

Extract and return only the research results, in plain text, WITHOUT any markup, markdown, additional formatting, or explanation.

SOURCE BUNDLE #F1
1. Quote: "Despite widespread use of traditional roof coatings, commercial buildings still lose an average of 33% of their cooling energy through the roof, with current coating strategies failing to adapt to changing climate conditions."

- Study analyzed 500 commercial buildings over 3 years

1. Quote: "Post-installation warranty extensions often create a false sense of security, with 64% of extended warranty claims being denied due to maintenance-related exclusions that weren't present in the original warranty terms."
2. Source: Commercial Construction Insurance Review
3. Link: [URL]
4. Key Points:
- Analysis of 2,000 warranty extension claims over 2019-2023

ARTICLE CONTEXT

The core concept of the article is [{{3.CoreConcept}}].

The title is [{{9.result}}].

And the focus is:  
{{3.`Section6Focus-OPTIMIZATION-STRATEGIES`}}

================
VALIDATION RULES
================

STEP 1: Domain Check

For each source bundle:

1. Extract the root domain using these rules:
   - Strip protocol (http://, https://)
   - Strip www. if present
   - Take everything up to the first / or end of string
   - Strip any port numbers
   - Remove the TLD (.com, .org, .net, etc)

2. The resulting root domain is INVALID if:
   - It contains any of these forbidden strings as part of any word:
     roof
     contractor
     guild
     service
     taylor
     build
     company
     install
     inc
     llc

Examples of domain extraction and validation:

Full URL → Extracted root domain → Verdict

https://www.cartarga.com/blog/commercial-roofing → cartarga → VALID

https://paint.org/sustainability/cool-roofs → paint → VALID  

https://blog.industryknowhow.com/research → industryknowhow → VALID

https://research-institute.edu/studies → research-institute → VALID

https://tracoroofing.com/case-study → tracoroofing → INVALID (contains "roof")

https://jonesbuilders.com/about → jonesbuilders → INVALID (contains "build")

https://www.roofr.com/blog → roofr → INVALID (contains "roof")

https://pro-service.net/contact → pro-service → INVALID (contains "service")

IF A ROOT DOMAIN IS INVALID, RETURN "CITATION NOT FOUND" and start over with STEP 1 for any additional source bundles.

---

STEP 2:
If multiple source bundles are VALID (they passed Step 1), choose the BEST ONE based on how well it fits the Article Context.

OUTPUT
========

If ZERO source bundles have valid root domains, return "CITATION NOT FOUND" and STOP HERE.

Otherwise, return the complete source bundle exactly as formatted in the input.
